mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367941M as of 2006-07-01 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 3+ months, monetary tightening restricts credit availability and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of 2006-07-01 indicates restrictive stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 3+ months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market tightening and wage pressure, which compresses profit margins and reduces equity valuations. The mechanism operates through rising input costs and reduced earnings growth expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% (revised to 4.7%) as of 2006-07-01 shows tight labor market
failure_condition: UNRATE < 4.5% fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates an overheating economy approaching capacity constraints. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market prices in future Fed tightening and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.21 and UNRATE at 4.7% suggest tight supply-labor conditions
failure_condition: Joint signal (INDPRO growth > 0.5% AND UNRATE < 4.8%) fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction demand and consumer wealth effects. Declining housing starts predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through reduced construction employment, lower consumer confidence, and diminished wealth-driven consumption.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1850K (revised to 1802K) as of 2006-06-01 shows potential softening
failure_condition: HOUST decline > 10% fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals healthy real wage growth and robust consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved earnings expectations and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.2% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135354K and CPIAUCSL at 202.3 show stable employment and moderate inflation
failure_condition: Joint signal (PAYEMS > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL < 0.3%) fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of Fed rate increases, which compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings multiples. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in response to inflation acceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.3 as of 2006-06-01 reflects moderate inflation environment
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals demand rotation away from housing toward other sectors, creating earnings uncertainty. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices sector valuations and growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND HOUST declines > 8% from 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11385.3B and HOUST at 1802K show growth with housing softening
regime_hint: demand rotation from housing to other sectors
failure_condition: Joint signal (GDPC1 > 2.5% AND HOUST decline > 8%) fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.
